text: Multi-query attention (MQA) uses a single key-value head in the decoder and therefore leads to faster decoder inference. Although the output quality of the resulting model may be degraded and faster inference of the current model may not be a motivation to train a separate new model, here we: (1) describe a simple “recipe” for uptraining a model using an existing multiday, multi-head language model checkpoint in less than 5% of the original training compute resources. (2) Introduce the term grouped query attention (GQA) to denote models using a nonunity number of key-value heads, larger than one and less than the number of the query heads as in multi-head attention, a generalization of the MQA, and demonstrate up-trained GQA having only a small loss in model quality compared to mult-head attention while having the faster inference of the corresponding models that use MQA.
